source_jobs.title: Accounts Receivable Associate - Collections - Corporate Office Irvine
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are 
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity, and professionalism coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live.
Action Property Management seeks a Accounts Receivable Associate – Collections for our corporate office located in Irvine, CA.
Job Summary 
Process accounts receivable and collections activities for multiple associations. Post late charges, review accounts, process pre liens, liens, lien releases and attorney packs. Perform preparation and detailed review of delinquency reports and collection activities.
 
Compensation: $25 to $28 per hour DOE
Schedule: Monday to Friday: 7:00 AM to 3:30 PM
The position will be 100% in office for the first 90 days and then it will be a hybrid schedule.

The Assistant General Manager acts as a liaison related to community information and member services and assists in the training and supervision of front desk and access control staff.



Action Property Management is seeking an Assistant General Manager to support the General Manager in leading operations at one of Austin's premier luxury high-rise communities. This is an outstanding opportunity for a motivated leader who is ready to make an impact while growing their career with an industry-leading management company.

source_jobs.title: Chief Building Engineer - Azzurra
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity and professionalism, coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation, and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 
 
Job Summary
The Chief Building Engineer plays a key leadership role in overseeing the maintenance, repair, and efficient operation of all building systems within a luxury high-rise community. This position manages the on-site engineering team, coordinates vendor work, and ensures compliance with safety regulations and building standards. The ideal professional is proactive, highly skilled, and takes pride in maintaining a best-in-class residential environment.
 
Compensation:  $115,000 – $125,000 annually
Schedule:  Monday – Friday, 7:00 a.m. – 3:30 p.m. (with 24/7 emergency availability)
 
Essential Job Duties and Responsibilities: 
Lead, train, and manage the onsite engineering and maintenance team, ensuring high performance and accountability.
Oversee the maintenance and operation of all mechanical, HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and life safety systems.
Conduct routine inspections, manage preventive maintenance programs, and ensure all systems operate at optimal efficiency.
Coordinate and supervise vendors and contractors, ensuring quality control and adherence to standards.
Assist with RFP preparation, bid evaluations, and capital improvement projects.
Maintain compliance with all local building codes, safety regulations, and HOA policies.
Respond promptly to emergencies and resident concerns with professionalism and composure.
Manage maintenance budgets, inventory, and supplies.
Foster collaboration and teamwork across all departments to ensure smooth community operations.
 
 
Qualifications / Requirements
·         Minimum 7 years of experience in building engineering or facilities management; luxury residential or high-rise experience preferred.
·         Minimum 3 years of supervisory experience leading maintenance or engineering staff.
·         Strong understanding of mechanical systems, diagnostics, and building automation controls.
·         Excellent organizational, leadership, and communication skills.
·         Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite and work order management systems.
·         Commitment to professionalism, teamwork, and superior service standards.
·         Must be available for emergency response outside regular hours.
 
 
Certifications/Licenses Required
·         High school diploma or equivalent required; college degree preferred.
·         HVAC Certification.
·         EPA/608 License.
·         Weekly Fire Certification preferred.
·         Fire Life Safety Director Certificate preferred.
 
Why You'll Love Working at Action
At Action Property Management, we believe in creating an environment where you truly love where you work. We offer competitive pay and ample opportunities for career growth and advancement. Our comprehensive benefits package includes medical, dental, vision, pet insurance, 401(k) with company match, and life and disability support for qualifying team members. We also provide generous paid time off, including vacation hours, sick time, and company-observed holidays for qualifying team members. We are committed to investing in the infrastructure, technology, training, and tools you need to excel in your role. Our relentless dedication to our company values and culture ensures a workplace where you feel heard, supported, and valued.  Join us

source_jobs.title: Community Manager (HOA) | Opportunities Throughout California
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity, and professionalism coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 

Job Description
Portfolio Community Managers generally manage 4 to 7 communities and are provided dedicated support from key departments. This allows our Community Managers to stay focused on their key objectives and offer a level of service, expertise and knowledge that surpasses our client's expectations.


Join a Team Where Great Community Managers Thrive!
Are you an experienced HOA Community Manager who thrives on building relationships, solving challenges, and creating exceptional homeowner experiences? If you're looking for a company that invests in its people, values leadership, and provides opportunities for career growth, we'd love to meet you.
We are actively seeking experienced Community Managers for current and future opportunities throughout the (NorCal) San Francisco Bay Area, and (SoCal) Los Angeles, Orange County, San Diego, and Inland Empire regions. If you're passionate about creating exceptional community experiences and partnering with Boards of Directors, we'd love to connect with you. 
We welcome candidates with experience in:
HOA / Community Association Management
Multifamily Property Management
Residential Property Management
Luxury Residential Communities
Condominium and High-Rise Managemen
Hospitality and Client-Facing Leadership Roles
Base Salary: $80k-$115k/year DOE, plus benefits!
 
*Please Note: This is an evergreen posting intended to build our talent pipeline for future Community Manager opportunities.
While there may not be an immediate opening at the time of your application, we are continuously seeking experienced professionals who align with our values and service standards. Applications submitted through this posting will be reviewed and retained for consideration as current and future opportunities become available within our Northern/Southern California portfolio.

source_jobs.title: Director of Accounting Operations
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity and professionalism, coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation, and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 
 
Job Summary
The Director, Accounting Operations is responsible for the execution and performance of HOA service models, including SnapHOA and similar portfolios, and oversees Accounts Receivable functions, including cash posting, collections, and receivables-related workflows. This role sits between Accounting and Operations and owns the intake, coordination, and flow of information required to support accurate financial reporting and consistent client service. The position ensures that operational activity is visible, structured, and aligned with accounting requirements, while improving service delivery, reducing risk, and standardizing workflows across accounts.


This is a hybrid position based out of the Irvine corporate office. 
 
Compensation: up to $145k depending upon experience

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering & Operations- The Infinity
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Job Summary
 
The Director of Engineering & Operations is responsible for the overall leadership, management, and performance of the Association’s physical plant, building systems, and operational services. This role oversees day-to-day building operations, long-term asset management, preventive maintenance programs, vendor and staff performance, regulatory compliance, and capital or operational projects.
This position combines hands-on engineering oversight with strategic operational leadership to ensure safe, efficient, cost-effective, and high-quality building operations while supporting residents, staff, Management, and the Board of Directors. 
 


Compensation: up to $150,000 depending upon experience 
 
Ideal Candidate MUST HAVE: HOA High Rise Experience, Capital Improvement Project experience, Construction, Vendor Management, Leadership and Budgeting Experience.

source_jobs.title: Director of Operations | Luxury High-Rise Residences- Millennium Tower
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity and professionalism, coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 
Bring Forbes-Level Hospitality Excellence to One of San Francisco's Most Prestigious Residential Communities
Have you built your career delivering exceptional experiences at a Forbes Four-Star or Five-Star luxury hotel, resort, or branded residence?
Do you thrive leading high-performing teams, elevating service standards, and creating unforgettable experiences for discerning guests and residents?
Summary:
Millennium Tower, one of San Francisco's most iconic luxury residential towers, is seeking an experienced Director of Operations to lead daily operations and help redefine luxury residential living through world-class hospitality and service excellence.

The Director of Operations is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operations of our prestigious luxury high-rise association, Millennium Tower, located in San Francisco, CA. The Director of Operations ensures seamless delivery of luxury services to residents. This role requires a strong background in luxury hotel or hotel residences, with a particular focus on rooms division operations. The ideal candidate will possess exceptional leadership skills, a keen eye for detail, and a commitment to delivering exceptional service. 
This is a unique opportunity for a Director of Rooms, Director of Residences, Director of Front Office, Hotel Manager, Assistant General Manager, or Operations Executive who is ready to bring Forbes-level service standards into an exclusive luxury residential environment.
Ideal candidate MUST HAVE: Forbes background/experience with strong background from luxury hotel or hotel residences experience from a Forbes 4-Star or 5-Star hotel/resort maintaining Forbes standards, and service level delivery.
 
Compensation: $110,000–$135,000 annual base salary depending on experience, plus eligibility for an annual discretionary performance bonus and holiday bonus!
 
Our comprehensive benefits package also includes complimentary parking, flexible scheduling, and additional employee perks such as dry-cleaning services. 
 
Schedule: 
This is a full-time onsite leadership role located at Millennium Tower in San Francisco.
Monday – Friday
9:00 AM – 5:30 PM
Because luxury service never stops, flexibility is required to support business needs, resident events, emergencies, and operational priorities, including participation in an alternating weekend leadership coverage rotation.

source_jobs.title: Front Desk Associate (Swing Shift ) - Savina
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity, and professionalism coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation, and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 
 
Action is seeking a Full-Time Front Desk Associate (Swing Shift) to join our team at our luxury association Savina in San Diego.
 
Under the direction of the assigned supervisor, the Front Desk Associate is the first point of contact for people entering the lobby or calling the association. As such, the Front Desk Associate must at all times display a professional appearance and speak in a polite manner and clear tone, including answering telephones or addressing people who have approached the Front Desk. The Front Desk Associate is expected to possess a working knowledge of the Association’s rules, policies and procedures, as well as familiarity with the Association’s amenities and surroundings in order to assist residents and their guests. 
 
Compensation: $21 to $23 per hour
 
Schedule: Friday to Tuesday; 3:00 PM to 11:30 PM

source_jobs.title: Front Desk Associate- Solair (Bilingual - Korean)
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity and professionalism, coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation, and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live.
 
Action is seeking a full-time Front Desk Associate to join our Solair team in Los Angeles.
 
Compensation:  $23.00 Hourly
Schedule: Monday - Friday 3:00pm - 11:00pm
 
Must be bilingual in Korean
 
What You’ll Do (Job Duties)
Represent Action Property Management and the Association by delivering exceptional 5-star customer service and ensuring 100% resident satisfaction.
Maintain the appearance of the front desk area, lobby, and main elevators.
Monitor lobby activity and maintain access control.
Provide concierge services.
Complete Daily Activity Report
Answer and direct incoming phone calls. Receive and distribute resident parcels.
Address resident and guest concerns and questions.
Demonstrate a strong understanding of the association's governing documents.
 
Who You Are (Skills/Experience)
Must be over 18 years of age and successfully pass a pre-employment background check and drug screening.
At least 1 year of customer service experience.
Highschool Diploma/GED or equivalent
Hospitality, luxury retail, or fine dining experience is preferred.
Hospitality Management college students or recent graduates are highly desired. Polished and professional appearance and demeanor
Upbeat and positive team player attitude
Strong judgment and solutions-oriented
Proactive customer service approach
 
Why You’ll Love Working at Action
 
At Action Property Management, we believe in creating an environment where you truly love where you work. We offer competitive pay and ample opportunities for career growth and advancement. Our comprehensive benefits package includes medical, dental, vision, pet insurance, 401(k) with company match, and life and disability support for qualifying team members. We also provide generous paid time off, including vacation hours, sick time, and company-observed holidays for qualifying team members. We are committed to investing in the infrastructure, technology, training, and tools you need to excel in your role. Our relentless dedication to our company values and culture ensures a workplace where you feel heard, supported, and valued.  Join us at Action Property Management and make a meaningful impact.
 
Action Property Management is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Supports a Drug Free Workplace. Pursuant to the San Francisco and Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

About the Role
The General Manager for Azzurra, serves as the trusted advisor to the Board of Directors and oversees all day-to-day operations of a luxury high rise residential community. The ideal candidate brings 5-7 years of HOA high rise onsite management, advanced project management expertise, deep financial acumen, and the proven ability to navigate complex issues, including litigation, structural defects, and multi-million-dollar budgets.
 
Compensation: up to $190k depending upon experience + comprehensive benefits package
Schedule: Monday -Friday business hours; available for evening board meetings and special events.

Job Summary

This role is pivotal in fostering strong relationships with homeowners, Board members, and on-site teams while ensuring the property’s standards align with Action’s core values and service excellence. The General Manager leads with integrity, strategic vision, and a commitment to continuous improvement, driving exceptional client satisfaction and operational efficiency.
 


Action Property Management, the leader in luxury HOA management, is seeking an experienced General Manager to oversee operations at one of downtown Austin's most iconic residential towers. This is an exceptional opportunity for a hospitality-minded leader who is passionate about delivering outstanding resident experiences, building high-performing teams, and partnering with an engaged Board of Directors.
If you thrive in fast-paced, luxury environments and have a proven track record leading high-rise communities, we'd love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Join Our Team!
source_jobs.description:
Join the Action Property Management Team!
At Action Property Management, our mission is simple; to help residents love where they live- and that starts with team members who love where they work. We're always on the hunt for passionate, service driven individuals to join our growing team. Whether your background is in hospitality, facilities management, administration or community management, we offer rewarding career opportunities where your work truly makes a difference. 

Who We Are
Action Property Management is one of the most trusted names in the HOA industry, delivering exceptional service to luxury residential communities across California and Texas. Built on a foundation of integrity, innovation, and people-first values, Action is more than a workplace—it’s a place to grow, connect, and thrive! 

Our Culture & Values:
 We believe the best resident experience begins with the employee experience. That’s why we seek individuals who align with our core values:
Teamwork – Collaborating with others to achieve shared goals
Excellence – Consistently delivering our best work
Innovation & Creativity – Embracing fresh ideas and continuous improvement
Genuine Care for People – Demonstrating empathy, respect, and kindness in all interactions

source_jobs.title: Join our Team! Seaholm Team (Current Employees Only)
source_jobs.description:
Join the Action Property Management Team
Now accepting applications from current onsite team members

Action Property Management is excited to welcome you! As part of this transition, we invite all current onsite team members who wish to continue serving the community to apply and join the Action team. 

We value the expertise, commitment, and relationships you’ve built within the community. Our goal is to provide a smooth, respectful, and supportive onboarding experience for everyone interested in continuing their career with us.
At Action Property Management, our mission is simple: help homeowners love where they live. That begins with supporting the talented individuals who make that happen every day.

How to Apply:
Please submit your application to express interest in continuing your employment at your community under Action Property Management. Once received, a recruiter will reach out to:
*Guide you through next steps
*Answer questions about the transition
*Provide information on benefits, onboarding, and expectations

We look forward to meeting you and welcoming you to the Action family!

Why You’ll Love Working at Action

At Action Property Management, we believe in creating an environment where you truly love where you work. We offer competitive pay and ample opportunities for career growth and advancement. Our comprehensive benefits package includes medical, dental, vision, pet insurance, 401(k) with company match, and life and disability support for qualifying team members. We also provide generous paid time off, including vacation hours, sick time, and company-observed holidays for qualifying team members. We are committed to investing in the infrastructure, technology, training, and tools you need to excel in your role. Our relentless dedication to our company values and culture ensures a workplace where you feel heard, supported, and valued.  Join us at Action Property Management and make a meaningful impact.
 
Action Property Management is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Supports a Drug Free Workplace. Pursuant to the San Francisco and Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

source_jobs.title: Join Our Team! Senior Portfolio Community Manager - Orange County
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity, and professionalism coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately owned HOA management company in the West, we proudly serve over 300 communities across 9 offices. Our success is fueled by a team of nearly 900 dedicated team members who genuinely love what they do and are dedicated to helping homeowners thrive and love where they live. 
 
Job Description
Community Managers generally manage 4 to 7 communities and are provided dedicated support from key departments. This allows our Community Managers to stay focused on their key objectives and offer a level of service, expertise and knowledge that surpasses our client's expectations.
 
 
Please Note: This is an evergreen posting intended to build our talent pipeline for future Community Manager opportunities. While there may not be an immediate opening at the time of your application, we are continuously seeking experienced professionals who align with our values and service standards. Applications submitted through this posting will be reviewed and retained for consideration as current and future opportunities become available within our Orange County portfolio.
What You Need
3-5 years of HOA portfolio management experience.
CMCA certification or the ability to obtain within your first 6 months of employment.
Evening availability for Board meetings.
Ability to travel to and from your property for in-person meetings when needed.
Keen knowledge of budgets and the budgeting process. 
Strong knowledge of audits, reserve studies, bidding process, and Association governing documents. 
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Effective presentation skills. 
Exceptional time management skills and ability to work independently. 
Commitment to following up on all issues in a timely manner.
How We Support You

source_jobs.title: Luxury Door Host (Valet/Front Desk) – Full Time – The Carlyle Residences
source_jobs.description:
Are you ready to step into the world of opulence and redefine legendary service?
 
Action Property Management is seeking an exceptional individual to join our elite team as a Luxury Door Host (Front Desk/Valet) at our prestigious luxury high-rise, The Carlyle Residences, located in Los Angeles. As one of the first points of contact for residents and guests, you will play a critical role in delivering a refined, five-star experience while setting the tone for service excellence throughout the property.
 
This position offers a clear growth path to Concierge, making it an ideal opportunity for hospitality professionals looking to advance within a luxury residential environment.
 
Compensation: $22.00 - $25.00 per hour
 
Schedule:Thursday - Monday, 12:30 pm–9:00 pm. 
 
Driving Responsibilities: Yes
 
Here are a few perks to joining this amazing team:
Career growth opportunities
Annual bonus based on tenure
Full uniform and dry cleaning provided
Flexible schedules
Fun, team-oriented environment including holiday celebrations, staff appreciation lunches, training, and mentoring
 
Why You’ll Love Working at Action
Birthday Holiday: Enjoy a paid day off to celebrate your birthday
One-on-One Mentoring: Personalized guidance and professional development support
10 Days’ Vacation: Generous time off to relax and recharge
Team Member Incentive Program: Earn rewards for your contributions
Comprehensive Health Benefits: Medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match, and life and disability coverage for qualifying team members
Pet Insurance: Coverage options for your furry companions
Year-End Bonus: Recognition for your dedication and performance
Employee Discounts: Retail, travel, and entertainment savings through LifeMart
 
 
What You’ll Do (Job Duties)
As a representative of Action Property Management and The Carlyle Residences, the Luxury Door Host delivers consistent five-star service and ensures a welcoming, polished arrival experience for all residents and guests. This role oversees the front driveway and entrance, directing incoming traffic and coordinating closely with valet operations to manage vehicle flow and service efficiency.
Responsibilities include escorting residents and guests into the building, assisting with luggage, packages, and personal items, and maintaining the cleanliness and professional appearance of the front door, driveway, and lobby areas. The role also includes valet duties such as parking, retrieving, and relocating vehicles.
 
While the primary responsibility is door and valet coverage, this position is cross-trained at the front desk and expected to step in for Concierge coverage when needed, including monitoring lobby activity, maintaining access control, answering phones, assisting residents, and managing package distribution. Permanent Concierge shifts may become available in the future based on staffing needs and performance.
 
Requirements
Must be over 18 years of age and successfully pass a pre-employment background check and drug screening
Minimum of 6 months of customer service experience; hospitality, luxury retail, or fine dining experience preferred
Must be willing to valet and cross-train across front desk, door host, and valet functions
Valid California Driver’s License required
Hospitality Management college students or recent graduates are highly desired
Polished and professional appearance and demeanor
Positive, team-oriented attitude
Strong judgment with a solutions-oriented mindset
Proactive and service-driven approach
 
Who We Are
With a legacy spanning four decades, Action Property Management has become the premier choice for homeowner’s association management. Founded in 1984, Action began with a single client and a vision to elevate ethical and professional standards in the HOA industry. Our unwavering commitment to integrity and professionalism, coupled with our core values of excellence, innovation, and care for people, continues to define us.  Today, as the largest privately o

Job Summary
The General Manager at Action Property Management is a dynamic leader who oversees the daily operations and overall management of a premier residential community. This role is pivotal in fostering strong relationships with homeowners, Board members, and on-site teams while ensuring the property’s standards align with Action’s core values and service excellence. The General Manager leads with integrity, strategic vision, and a commitment to continuous improvement, driving exceptional client satisfaction and operational efficiency.

source_jobs.title: Luxury Residential Bell Person- Millennium Tower
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
For nearly two decades, the Millennium Tower service protocol has defined how the world's top luxury hotel brands serve their residents. Millennium's residential service has become the standard to which every other development aspires. From Valet to Bell Person to Concierge and beyond, you'll become part of providing a level of service that is unsurpassed in the luxury hospitality industry. Previously named one of the top ten luxury residential buildings in the world by Worth Magazine, Millennium Tower is a place where the true definition of an exclusive lifestyle is delivered and trends are born. At Millennium Tower, we foster a culture of excellence, where your skills and talents are nurtured and celebrated. We value hard work and dedication and reward top performers with competitive pay plus substantial performance bonuses ranging from $7,000 to $20,000.
 
Action Property Management is seeking a full-time Luxury Residential Bell Person to join our team at our association, Millennium Tower, in San Francisco. 
 
The responsibilities focus on deliveries to units such as luggage, packages, groceries, messages, etc. Additionally, the Bell Person will walk the property, they are to assist in picking up and/or cleaning any loose debris or trash that they may find and alert the Concierges of any condition demanding attention, ie., Maintenance Requests. The Bell Person will also cover the breaks of the Concierge and Door Host.
 
Compensation: $21.00- $25.00 Per Hour (Depending on Experience)+ Covered Benefits + Holiday Bonus + Annual Bonus ranging anywhere from $7,000 to $20,000
 
Schedule: 5 Days Per Week; 6:00 AM- 2:30 PM, 10:45 AM - 7:15PM and 2:00 PM- 10:30 PM; Weekends Required  
 
*Schedule is not guaranteed and is subject to change based on operational needs*

notes: Property management company with a large number of non-tech job postings
